title: emit-in-transaction
manager: Emission is transactional — `emit c <- expr` buffers the output and flushes it only when the macrostep commits, so an output caused by a step that then rolls back never escapes. This is what makes FSL's outputs trustworthy: the world only ever sees emissions from steps that actually happened, never from a transaction that was undone — the difference between exactly-once and an at-least-once mess someone downstream has to de-duplicate.
cs: `emit c <- expr` buffers in the emission set E, flushes at commit (C2 macrostep); rolled back with the step that caused it; the output dual of the transactional rollback (W6.69).
